LAW AND MOTION 302, HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF DEFENDANT/JUDGMENT DEBTOR ALGEREESE CLAYTON. THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS DENIED. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS DIRECTED TO RELEASE ANY FUNDS HELD TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR FOR PAYMENT ON THE JUDGMENT. DEBTOR HAS AGREED TO PAY 150 DOLLARS A MONTH BEGINNING NOVEMBER 15, 2019.(AS FULLY REPORTED IN THE REPORTER'S NOTES/TRANSCRIPT) ORDER IS SIGNED. (SEE SCANNED ORDER FOR DETAILS) JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: M. GOODMAN, COURT REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR#8600, MARIA.TORREANO@GMAIL.COM, REPORTED. =(302/EPS)

calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Oct-23-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Vanessa Thomas, Esq. 916-920-2295 of Nelson & Kennard for Plaintiff through Court Call.
Algereese Clayton, Esq. 415-912-9650 Defendant in Pro Per.

RULING - A R G U E D. Hearing is held. The Court asks the status of the meet and confer and if an agreement has been reached. Defendant reports to the Court. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION OF DEFENDANT/JUDGMENT DEBTOR ALGEREESE CLAYTON.

The Judgment Debtor's Claim of Exemption is Denied. The levying officer is directed to release any funds held to the Judgment Creditor for payment on the Judgment. Debtor has agreed to pay 150 dollars a month beginning November 15, 2019.(as fully reported in the reporter's notes/transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)

HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar Oct-16-2019 Continued To Oct-23-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Alan J. Sherwood, Esq., T:510.409.6199, specially appearing for the plaintiff/moving party;
Algereese P. Clayton, T:415.912.9650, defendant/claimant appearing on her own behalf, by Court Call.

RULING: Hearing held. The Court examined Ms. Clayton, judgment debtor/claimant, as to the source of the funds that were levied, her own source of income, and her monthly expenses. Defendant claims the levied money is not hers but belongs to her mother; plaintiff's counsel argued that the funds are in the defendant's bank account, not exempt and therefore could be subject to levy. After hearing, Court ruled as follows:

PLAINTIFF FORD MOTOR CREDIT COMPANY's HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is continued for further hearing to October 23, 2019 at 9:30am in Department 302. Parties are to meet and confer regarding 1) a payment plan for the underlying debt and 2) the disposition of the levied funds that is the subject of this hearing.

Order continuing the hearing signed. The clerk shall serve a copy of the order on all parties, including a certified copy to the levying officer.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-02-2005 Off Calendar
